key_metrics: Gross profit of $2.24 billion, up 5.4% YoY
Operating, selling, general and administrative expenses increased 8.7% YoY to $1.54 billion
Share repurchases of $741.7 million over the 24-week period
qoq_changes: Net sales decreased sequentially from implied Q1 2026 ($4.63 billion) to Q2 2026 ($4.27 billion)
Merchandise inventories increased to $7.48 billion from $7.03 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year
new_risks: Margin compression risk as SG&A expense growth (8.7%) outpaced gross profit growth (5.4%)
Rising inventory levels ($7.48 billion) tying up working capital and potentially increasing carrying costs
management_tone: Neutral to cautious, as strong top-line revenue growth was overshadowed by rising operating costs that compressed margins and drove a year-over-year decline in net income.
analyst_note: While AutoZone demonstrated resilient demand with an 8.1% increase in net sales, profitability was pressured by rising SG&A expenses, resulting in a 3.9% drop in net income. Investors should monitor operating cost controls in upcoming quarters, though the company's robust share repurchase program continues to provide a floor for EPS.
